title: Hierarchy of Logical Equivalence Laws
description: A visual representation of the fundamental laws of logic used to prove the equivalence of compound statements, categorized by their type and function.
elements: Identity Laws (p ∧ T ≡ p, p ∨ F ≡ p)
Domination Laws (p ∨ T ≡ T, p ∧ F ≡ F)
Idempotent Laws (p ∨ p ≡ p, p ∧ p ≡ p)
Commutative Laws (p ∨ q ≡ q ∨ p, p ∧ q ≡ q ∧ p)
Associative Laws ((p ∨ q) ∨ r ≡ p ∨ (q ∨ r), (p ∧ q) ∧ r ≡ p ∧ (q ∧ r))
Distributive Laws (p ∨ (q ∧ r) ≡ (p ∨ q) ∧ (p ∨ r), p ∧ (q ∨ r) ≡ (p ∧ q) ∨ (p ∧ r))
Absorption Laws (p ∨ (p ∧ q) ≡ p, p ∧ (p ∨ q) ≡ p)
relationships: Laws are foundational tools used to transform and simplify logical expressions
They form a toolkit for proving two statements are logically equivalent without truth tables
Laws can be combined and applied sequentially to reach a desired equivalent form
